headline: AT&T (NYSE:T) Reports Sales Below Analyst Estimates In Q2 CY2026 Earnings
summary: Telecommunications conglomerate AT&T (NYSE:T) fell short of the market’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2026 as sales rose 2.3% year on year to $31.56 billion. Its GAAP profit of $0.66 per share was 11.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.summary: Telecommunications conglomerate AT&T (NYSE:T) fell short of the market’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2026 as sales rose 2.3% year on year to $31.56 billion. Its GAAP profit of $0.66 per share was 11.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: AT&T tops targets for wireless subscriber additions as bundle offers gain traction
summary: AT&T added more wireless subscribers than expected in the second quarter, as its revamped ‌low-cost, unlimited plans along with bundled mobile and ‌broadband offerings attracted value-conscious customers.  The gains build on AT&T's broader convergence strategy of selling multiple connectivity services to the same household to reduce churn and increase customer lifetime value as ‌U.S. telecom providers chase ⁠the same finite pool of users.  AT&T in March launched OneConnect, a single subscription that bundles ⁠unlimited wireless service with home internet under one monthly bill.

The gains build on AT&T's broader convergence strategy of selling multiple connectivity services to the same household to reduce churn and increase customer lifetime value as ‌U.S. telecom providers chase ⁠the same finite pool of users.  AT&T in March launched OneConnect, a single subscription that bundles ⁠unlimited wireless service with home internet under one monthly bill.

headline: AT&T Delivers Strong Second-Quarter Results as Investment-Led Strategy Gains Momentum
summary: AT&T Inc. (NYSE: T) reported strong second-quarter results, driven by consistent execution of the Company's investment-led strategy, demonstrating improved growth in consolidated service revenue and profitability. The Company continues to grow its base of high-value converged customers as it delivered a record quarter for combined fiber and fixed wireless net adds and its strongest consumer postpaid wireless account growth in more than three years.

headline: AT&T tops targets for new wireless subscribers as bundle offers spur demand
summary: AT&T topped quarterly estimates for wireless subscriber additions on Wednesday as cheaper unlimited plans paired ‌with bundled mobile-broadband offerings drew in value-conscious customers, sending its shares ‌up 4%.  The gains build on AT&T's broader convergence strategy of selling multiple connectivity services to ​the same household to reduce churn and increase customer lifetime value as U.S. telecom providers chase the same finite pool of users.  The bundled offerings also helped the company post record broadband additions, with 367,000 new fiber internet users and ‌279,000 fixed wireless subscribers.

content.title: AT&T tops targets for new wireless subscribers as bundle offers spur demand

The gains build on AT&T's broader convergence strategy of selling multiple connectivity services to ​the same household to reduce churn and increase customer lifetime value as U.S. telecom providers chase the same finite pool of users.  The bundled offerings also helped the company post record broadband additions, with 367,000 new fiber internet users and ‌279,000 fixed wireless subscribers.

headline: Oil prices rise another 3%, while Wall Street drifts in mixed trading
summary: The U.S. stock market is drifting in mixed trading Wednesday, while oil prices climb another 3% as fighting continues in the war with Iran.  Philip Morris International rose 3% after the seller of Marlboro cigarettes reported stronger profit and revenue for the latest quarter than expected.  CEO John Stankey also said the telecom is accelerating plans to send roughly $10 billion to its shareholders this year through the buybacks of its stock.

content.title: Oil prices rise another 3%, while Wall Street drifts in mixed trading
content.summary: The U.S. stock market is drifting in mixed trading Wednesday, while oil prices climb another 3% as fighting continues in the war with Iran.  Philip Morris International rose 3% after the seller of Marlboro cigarettes reported stronger profit and revenue for the latest quarter than expected.  CEO John Stankey also said the telecom is accelerating plans to send roughly $10 billion to its shareholders this year through the buybacks of its stock.

thumbnail.caption: Options traders Justin Kanda, Doran Swan, and Ravi Bhandari. left to right, work on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange, Thursday, June 25, 2026. (AP Photo/Richard Drew)
